description: Figure 8 presents TEM analyses of deformation-induced microstructures in an Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel at engineering strains of 6% (a–d) and 12% near final failure (e–h). Panels (a) and (e) are bright-field (BF) images showing increased dislocation density in both austenite and B2 phases with strain, with dislocations forming nonplanar slip bands and pile-ups around B2 particles. Panels (b) and (f) are dark-field (DF) images with selected-area diffraction patterns (SADP) inset, revealing attenuated superlattice reflections and reduced volume fraction of L1′2-type long-range ordered (LRO) domains within slip bands. Panels (c) and (g) show inverse FFT images confirming that dislocations shear through LRO domains, progressively destroying them. Panels (d) and (h) illustrate that coherent VC precipitates impede dislocation motion and cause dislocation accumulation, with the hindering effect becoming more pronounced at 12% strain. The figure demonstrates that austenite/B2 interfaces block planar slip and reflect dislocations, while dual-nanoprecipitation governs strain-hardening via dislocation shearing and pinning mechanisms.
